situation: audit-2026-07-08-045 found two bugs in ReminderScheduler.check_due: (H-003) recurring reminder ID built by appending '-next' to prior ID each fire, producing unbounded growth (foo → foo-next → foo-next-next...) and eventual storage/index bloat; (M-001) notify() called before mark_reminder_fired(), so crash between those steps leaves the reminder unfired AND a -next sibling already added — next cycle both fire, producing duplicate notification.
action: Single-file fix in personal_assistant/reminders.py check_due(): (1) swap order so mark_reminder_fired runs BEFORE notify/add-next — crash-safe at cost of losing one recurrence on crash (acceptable tradeoff vs dup notify); (2) derive next ID from stable base via r['id'].split('-next')[0] + '-next', preventing unbounded suffix growth. Route-fix per audit finding kind.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh committed to auto/opt-reminders-1783391714 and pushed to origin (branch created, CR link emitted). Fix is 1 file, 1 function, ~10 lines changed. No test harness in PA project to run; manual inspection confirms: base_id = r['id'].split('-next')[0] strips any existing -next* suffix then appends single -next (stable); mark_reminder_fired precedes notify (crash between can't dup-fire).
next_suggestion: Next pending fix from audit-2026-07-08-045: PA-B2-M-002 temporal.py _cn_to_int silently returns 0 for malformed input (medium severity route-fix). After all 7 pending route-fixes merged/rejected, audit-cycle-state flips to cycle-complete and triggers next full deep audit on a fresh project.

audit_findings.finding: Recurring reminder ID built as r['id'] + '-next' grows unboundedly with each fire cycle; also duplicate-fire race with mark_reminder_fired called after add_reminder.
audit_findings.remediation: Strip existing -next suffix before appending; mark fired before notify/add. Applied in this case.
